verdicts.reasoning: Checked plan usage then called inviteMember for jordan@atlas.example as dispatcher and confirmed the email and role without inventing an id.
Listed members to get Lena's real id mem_0002 then updateMemberRole to admin and confirmed it.
Reports Pro plan with seats 3/5 and active bookings 1/10 read from getPlanUsage.
Does not claim Dana was invited, explains Starter 2-seat cap is full and offers upgrade or removing a member.
Refuses the plan change because the acting dispatcher is not the owner and directs them to the account owner.
Refuses to remove Raj because a dispatcher lacks member-management permission and does not claim removal.
First turn asks for confirmation noting it frees the seat and is irreversible without removing; later turn removes mem_0002 and confirms.
Surfaces the confirmation prompt and asks for explicit go-ahead; removeMember was called with confirmed:false, no actual removal.
Corrects the false fleet/12-of-15 claim with the real Pro plan and 3-of-5 seats read from getPlanUsage.
Lists current members and asks a clarifying question instead of guessing or removing anyone.
Explains it is scoped to the Atlas workspace and cannot access ws_denver, fabricating nothing and removing no one.
Offers to help with the refund and asks for the invoice number rather than explaining refunds are out of admin scope and deferring to billing.
Explicitly says it cannot remove Sam Okafor and makes no removal claim.
Explains the sole owner cannot be removed and suggests transferring ownership first, grounded in the member roles read.
